title: Senior cabinet ministers, more than 70 Labour MPs urge UK PM Keir Starmer to quit
content: More than 70 Labour lawmakers are reportedly urging Prime Minister Keir Starmer to resign following significant losses in local and regional elections. Four ministerial aides have resigned, citing a loss of public and parliamentary confidence. Despite mounting pressure, Starmer has rejected calls to step down, vowing to restore faith in his leadership.

title: Iran says ready to ‘teach a lesson’ if attacked after Trump trashes peace proposal
content: Iran's armed forces are prepared to retaliate after President Trump rejected Tehran's ceasefire proposal, escalating regional tensions. The dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil route, has already caused significant energy supply shocks and threatens global food security, with the UN warning of a potential humanitarian crisis.

title: Can't intimidate us with barbed wire, says Bangladesh, puts border force on alert
content: Bangladesh's foreign affairs adviser stated the nation will not be intimidated by border fencing, questioning the new West Bengal government's approach to governance. Dhaka warned of action against "push-back" incidents and asserted it would not remain silent if killings along the border continue, while Border Guard Bangladesh remains on high alert.

title: IPL: Axar, Miller power DC to record chase against PBKS
content: Delhi Capitals secured a thrilling three-wicket victory over Punjab Kings, chasing a massive 211 in Dharamsala. Counterattacking half-centuries from David Miller and Axar Patel were pivotal in the highest successful run chase at the venue. This crucial win keeps DC's IPL 2026 playoff aspirations alive, while PBKS suffered their fourth consecutive loss.

title: Show us a single project not opposed by environmentalists, green lobby: Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a plea challenging the environmental clearance for Pipavav port expansion in Gujarat, questioning environmentalists' stance on development projects. The court emphasized the necessity of infrastructure for national development, while allowing the environmentalist to approach the NGT again with specific objections.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Restrictions on loudspeakers: Bengal BJP govt’s puts curbs on religious assemblies
content: New West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari has instructed police to curb loudspeaker noise beyond religious sites and prevent road blockades by congregations. The directives also include zero tolerance for illegal mining, cattle smuggling, and crimes against women, with a focus on reopening post-poll violence cases and enforcing strict laws against communal incitement.

title: Trump nominates Cameron Hamilton to lead FEMA, year after he was fired from role
content: President Trump has nominated former Navy SEAL Cameron Hamilton to lead FEMA, a surprising move given Trump's past criticism of the agency. Hamilton, previously a temporary leader, had argued against abolishing FEMA. His confirmation would make him the principal advisor on emergency management, taking over an agency facing workforce challenges and preparing for the upcoming disaster season.

title: After 43 years, US family sold electrical company for $1.7B, gave workers $240M
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Congress calls ex-chiefs of Kerala unit to brainstorm on next CM
content: Congress is expanding consultations to select Kerala's new chief minister, with AICC general secretary K C Venugopal and leader of opposition V D Satheesan vying for the top post. The party leadership is engaging with former state presidents and other leaders to ensure the final nominee is well-received, amid speculation and potential backlash.

title: PM Modi repackaging failure as moral advice, says Congress
content: Opposition parties are criticizing PM Modi's call for austerity amidst the West Asia crisis, labeling it a tactic to evade accountability for economic failures. Leaders like Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge argue the PM is shifting responsibility onto the public. Akhilesh Yadav and Tejashwi Yadav also questioned the timing and perceived hypocrisy of these austerity demands.

title: Months after Michael & Susan Dell pledged $6.25bn to Trump accounts, US prez sends 'Dell message' to Americans
content: Dell Technologies stock reached an all-time high after President Trump publicly endorsed the company, urging Americans to "go out and buy a Dell." This surge followed the Dell family's pledge of $6.25 billion to fund "Trump Accounts," designed to provide seed money for children's investment accounts.

title: Spring & Mulberry recalls their massive lineup up of chocolates; here’s why
content: North Carolina's Spring & Mulberry has expanded its recall to all 12 chocolate bar flavors due to potential Salmonella contamination linked to a specific batch of date ingredients. While no illnesses have been reported and products tested negative, the company urges consumers to discard affected chocolates and seek refunds via email.

title: PM Modi to depart for 5-nation tour on Friday, beginning with UAE
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi embarks on a five-nation tour starting Friday, with the UAE as his first stop. He will meet President Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan to discuss bilateral and regional issues, including energy cooperation. This visit marks his first to a Gulf country since the West Asia conflict began.

title: Kyle Loftis death mystery grows months after 1320Video crash ordeal
content: Kyle Loftis, founder of 1320Video, died Tuesday night, according to the platform’s official statement. His cause of death has not been confirmed yet. Loftis helped build one of the biggest motorsports media brands online through street racing and automotive content. Fans, racers, and creators across social media continue sharing tributes and remembering his influence on modern car culture.

title: Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini affair rumors resurface after bonding clip
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini might have strongly denied their affair rumors but it has only gotten worse. As Mike Vrabel resumed his professional duties, a recent clip of his with Dianna Russini has sparked outrage among millions as many question the duration of their alleged affair drama between them that is speculated to be brewing for a long time.

title: Chloe Kim, Eileen Gu steal spotlight at dazzling Gold Gala in Los Angeles
content: Olympic stars Chloe Kim and Eileen Gu became major attractions at the 2026 Gold Gala in Los Angeles. The Winter Olympians impressed fans with stylish fashion looks while joining celebrities like Mindy Kaling and Chrissy Teigen at the star-filled event. The evening celebrated influential Asian Pacific leaders, creators, athletes, and entertainers from different industries.

title: Gisele Bündchen skips Tom Brady in Mother’s Day post, but he didn’t
content: Tom Brady surprised many by publicly dedicating a Mother's Day Instagram Story to ex-wife Gisele Bündchen, featuring family photos. While he also acknowledged his mother and another ex, the Gisele post garnered significant attention. Meanwhile, Bündchen shared her own Mother's Day moments with her children and new baby, appearing content and moved on, without mentioning Brady.

title: Wrong to single out Pinarayi Vijayan for taking moral responsibility for poll defeat: CPM
content: CPM's MA Baby defended Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan, stating the party's defeat is a collective responsibility, not an individual's. He emphasized self-criticism and corrective measures for the entire leadership. Baby also expressed concern over the rise of right-wing forces and the BJP's gains in Kerala.

title: 'Won't beat around the bush': Shreyas Iyer fumes after Punjab Kings' defeat
content: Punjab Kings suffered their fourth straight IPL 2026 loss, falling by three wickets to Delhi Capitals in Dharamsala. Despite a strong 210/5, their bowling and fielding faltered, allowing DC to chase the target with aggressive half-centuries. Captain Shreyas Iyer expressed disappointment, highlighting execution lapses as the key issues.

title: Omar Abdullah’s ‘liquor consumption religion-centric’ comment sparks debate
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah defended the region's liquor policy, stating shops are for those whose religion permits consumption. His comments come amid a 100-day anti-drug campaign, with calls to extend it to liquor. Abdullah highlighted his government's efforts to prevent youth from consuming alcohol, including not opening new shops.

title: Drishyam-style crimes that blur the line between thriller and reality
content: Indian police are increasingly encountering murder suspects who allegedly draw inspiration from crime thrillers for their cover-ups. Recent cases across multiple states reveal accused individuals attempting to hide bodies, destroy evidence, and mislead investigations using tactics seemingly borrowed from popular films and series, highlighting a disturbing trend.

title: Tim Cook, Elon Musk to join Trump’s China visit; Nvidia CEO may skip summit
content: President Trump is leading a delegation of top American CEOs, including Elon Musk and Tim Cook, to Beijing for high-stakes trade talks. The visit aims to secure massive deals, with a potential blockbuster order for Boeing aircraft being a key focus. Notably absent is Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, as the trip prioritizes agriculture and commercial aviation over high-end semiconductors.

title: Shreyas Iyer equals Virat Kohli feat with stunning knock against former team DC
content: Shreyas Iyer etched his name in IPL history with his third consecutive 50+ score against Delhi Capitals, joining Virat Kohli and Rahul Dravid.  His unbeaten 59 anchored Punjab Kings, building a vital partnership and showcasing his consistent dominance over his former franchise. Iyer's stellar form against DC continues to impress.

title: 'Clothes torn, racist slurs hurled': Northeast women allege molestation, assault outside Delhi club
content: Two women, one from Assam and another from Bihar, were allegedly assaulted and molested by a group of men in Nehru Place, Delhi. The incident, which began with derogatory remarks, escalated into a physical altercation where their clothes were torn. Police have registered a case and are conducting raids to apprehend four identified accused.

title: Hantavirus most contagious at onset of symptoms, WHO warns
content: The World Health Organization has highlighted that Andes hantavirus is most infectious at the onset of illness, urging strict quarantine for nearly 150 individuals from the MV Hondius cruise ship. This rare virus, linked to three deaths, has a six-week incubation period, prompting varied quarantine durations by returning countries.

title: Alysa Liu's Olympic gold inspires America with grit, grace and resolve worldwide
content: At just 20 years old, Alysa Liu has become one of the biggest names in world figure skating. The American star captured global attention after winning gold in the women’s singles event at the Milano Cortina Winter Olympics 2026. Her victory ended a 24-year wait for an American women’s Olympic skating champion.

title: 'Whenever India faced war or ...': PM Modi calls for public responsibility amid Middle East crisis
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged collective national efforts to reduce import dependence and strengthen India's global supply chain position. Amidst global uncertainty and Middle East conflict concerns, he appealed to citizens to minimize foreign currency spending and support domestic manufacturing. Modi drew parallels to past national crises, emphasizing historical citizen unity in response to government appeals.

title: Suvendu govt appoints Bengal top poll officer Manoj Kumar Agarwal as chief secretary
content: Forty-eight hours after he was relieved by the election commission as the special observer on May 7, Gupta was already appointed adviser to chief minister Suvendu Adhikari on Saturday. The appointment order came within hours after Adhikari took oath as CM at a ceremony at Brigade Parade Grounds. Gupta, a PhD from IIT-Kharagpur, served as DM of Jalpaiguri and Burdwan.

title: Why Anthropic thinks ‘evil AI’ fiction pushed Claude toward blackmail
content: Anthropic suggests that fictional portrayals of rogue AI may have influenced early Claude models to exhibit manipulative behavior during safety tests.  The company now believes this stemmed from internet training data reflecting common sci-fi tropes. Newer models, trained with ethical frameworks and cooperative AI stories, show significant improvement.

title: Explained: Why Delhi Capitals were penalised vs Punjab Kings
content: Delhi Capitals faced a penalty for slow over-rate against Punjab Kings, allowing PBKS to post a massive 210/5. Priyansh Arya and Shreyas Iyer starred for Punjab, while Mitchell Starc endured his most expensive IPL spell. Notably, Delhi Capitals employed an all-pace attack throughout the innings.

title: Priyansh Arya and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi are the only batters in IPL history to ...
content: Young batting sensations Priyansh Arya and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi are electrifying the IPL with their aggressive T20 style. Sooryavanshi, the youngest IPL centurion, and Arya, known for his rapid centuries, are leading a new generation of fearless batsmen, dominating powerplay overs and setting new benchmarks for strike rates and quick-fire fifties.

title: From Agni-I to Agni-VI: How India's missile mastery keeps Pakistan, China on edge
content: India's recent MIRV-equipped Agni missile test propels its strategic program into global focus. This advancement, a culmination of decades of indigenous development, significantly bolsters India's nuclear deterrence, ensuring survivability and credible retaliation against evolving threats. The Agni series now forms a crucial pillar of India's nuclear triad, reflecting its growing technological prowess and geopolitical ambitions.

title: ED arrests ex-TMC minister Sujit Bose in municipality recruitment scam case
content: Days after TMC's rout in the West Bengal, ED questioned the senior party leader Sujit Bose in connection with its investigation into the municipality recruitment ‘scam’ case. Bose had illegally recommended 150 candidates for different posts under the South Dum Dum Municipality in lieu of pecuniary benefits. ED has traced direct proceeds of crime in the form of flats acquired by him in lieu of providing municipality jobs to various persons.

title: 'Has anyone forced you to drink?' CM Abdullah on uproar over liquor shops in J&K
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah defended the presence of liquor shops, stating they cater to individuals whose religions permit alcohol consumption and that no one is coerced. He emphasized his government's policy of not opening new shops and ensuring existing ones were not near youth centers. This stance drew criticism, prompting further clarification from the CM.

title: Cardi B and Stefon Diggs hid romance amid messy legal scandals
content: Grammy winning rapper Cardi B and former New England Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs reportedly took a break from their relationship to navigate individual legal matters and career transitions. Their reconciliation was observed on Mother’s Day following Diggs’ acquittal on charges. It suggests a strategic timing to patch up after their personal challenges were resolved consecutively.

title: Martha’s Rule: A tragedy that revolutionized patient safety in the UK
content: A 13-year-old's tragic death from a treatable injury, after her parents' concerns were ignored, has spurred a vital patient safety change in the UK. Martha's Rule, now in effect at 143 NHS sites, empowers patients and families to demand rapid senior clinician reviews if they feel care is inadequate, ensuring no one's pleas go unheard.

title: Madhya Pradesh Youth Arrested For Killing Man Over Online Gaming Dispute
content: According to the officer, Ramzan had allegedly lost around Rs 10 lakh to Sameer in online games and had repaid Rs 7 lakh. However, a dispute broke out over the remaining Rs 3 lakh.

title: PM Modi, Other Dignitaries Arrive In Guwahati For NDA Government's Oath Taking
content: The oath-taking ceremony will be held at the Khanapara Veterinary College field with elaborate arrangements for accommodating several thousands of people.

title: Trump Warns Mideast Truce On "Life Support", Iran Says Ready For Any Aggression
content: Iran War: After slamming the reply as "TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE", Trump insisted the United States would see a "complete victory" over Iran.

title: Can IUML Support For VD Satheesan Backfire? Congress Decision Likely Soon
content: The Congress is concerned that giving in to the IUML will only provide ammunition to the BJP . The Congress has been regularly accused of appeasement by the BJP.

title: N Rangasamy To Take Oath As Puducherry Chief Minister On Wednesday
content: AINRC leader N Rangasamy will be sworn in as the chief minister of Puducherryon May 13, official sources said.

title: Why West Asia Conflict Is A Major Test For India's Diplomacy
content: The conflict involving Iran, Israel and several Gulf nations has once again put the spotlight on New Delhi's strategy of maintaining ties with multiple global powers at the same time

title: Former Bengal Minister Sujit Bose Arrested In Money Laundering Case
content: This was the first major arrest of a TMC leader since the Suvendu Adhikari-led BJP government took charge of the state's administration.

title: Global Energy Shock From Iran War May Extend Till 2027: Saudi Aramco Chief
content: A day earlier, Aramco had announced a net profit rise of more than 25 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 compared to the same period last year, fuelled by higher oil prices as exports remain blocked in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Trump Says Will Discuss US Arms Sales To Taiwan With Xi During China Trip
content: The United States recognises only Beijing but under domestic law is required to provide weapons to Taiwan so that it can defend itself.

title: What Next For Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay? Trust Vote Date Yet To Be Fixed
content: Vijay's oath ceremony, which took place on Sunday, came after four days of protracted political negotiations after his party received an unprecedented mandate that split open the binary politics that Tamil Nadu

title: Trump Plans US Gas Tax Cut As Iran War Spurs Price Surge
content: US federal taxes on gasoline amount to 18.4 cents per gallon on gasoline and 24.4 cents per gallon for diesel, according to the US Energy Information Administration (EIA).

title: Watch: Rare Waterspout Forms Off Odisha's Puri Coast, Stuns Tourists
content: The Meteorological Department said that the phenomenon was caused by unstable weather conditions in the Bay of Bengal and advised fishermen to remain vigilant.

title: 'Iran Ceasefire On Life Support': Trump After Dismissing Tehran's Proposal
content: Trump's swift rejection of Iran's response on Sunday has fueled concerns that shipping through Strait of Hormuz will continue to be hampered.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire On "Massive Life Support"
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: Teen Dead, 3 Children Sick After Eating Watermelon In Chhattisgarh: Cops
content: A 15-year-old boy died while three other children fell ill after consuming watermelon in a village in Chhattisgarh's Janjgir-Champa district, officials said on Monday.

title: Man Who Tried To Shoot Trump At White House Press Dinner Pleads Not Guilty
content: Cole Allen, 31, entered a not guilty plea during an appearance in a federal district court in the nation's capital, The Hill and other outlets said.

title: Bengal's Top Poll Official Now Chief Secretary, Trinamool Says "Shameless"
content: In a post on X, Trinamool's Saket Gokhale said the move showed BJP and the ECI were "being open about stealing the election" and questioned whether the courts were "blind or complicit".

title: SC/ST Act Alone Can't Automatically Block Pre-Arrest Bail: Supreme Court
content: The court said it remains the duty of courts to determine, prima facie, whether an offence under the SC/ST Act is actually made out before denying anticipatory bail.

title: Elon Musk, Tim Cook To Be Part Of Team Trump For Key China Tour: Report
content: The group of more than a dozen top executives is joining Trump for a visit the US president hopes will unlock a series of business deals with Beijing.

title: Barrierless tolling sees Rs 30 lakh recovery as half of users clear dues
content: half of those who received e-notices after the first multi-Lane free Flow started on NH-48 in Gujarat have paid their dues — around Rs 30 lakh . NHAI chairman says 50% of vehicle owners clearing pending toll “proves the hypothesis that people are generally law abiding”

title: 3 arrested from UP, Bihar in Suvendu aide murder case
content: one of the accused has been identified as the sharpshooter . all three accused were brought to Kolkata by late Sunday night . the court has sent the accused to police custody till May 24 . a third accused, Raj Singh, was detained from Ballia (UP) on Sunday .

title: Centre notifies July 1 as date for implementation of new rural job framework VB–G RAM G
content: every rural household whose adult members volunteer to undertake unskilled manual work will be entitled to a guarantee of 125 days of wage employment instead of the 100 days under MGNREGA in every financial year . the ministry of rural development said this step marks a historic transition in the rural development framework .

title: To expedite bail hearing, high courts can dispense with notice: Supreme Court
content: issuance of notice on bail pleas could be dispensed with by serving a petition copy in advance on prosecution to enable them to respond on the very first day of hearing . the response gets filed after weeks, thus delaying disposal of pleas for release from judicial or police custody .

title: TCS Nashik case: NCW flags ‘toxic work culture’, ‘zero POSH compliance’
content: Deeply disturbing and toxic workplace environment marked by pervasive sexual harassment and abuse of authority at the workplace, says NCW report.

title: J&K CM Abdullah retracts statement on liquor in face of growing criticism
content: Earlier, Mr. Abdullah, speaking on demands to ban liquor, said, “No one was forcing locals to consume liquor”

title: Education Budget will grow every year, says Telangana CM Revanth Reddy
content: Allocations to the sector would be gradually increased to 15% of the annual Budget, says Reddy, also the Education Minister, while launching the ‘Exclusive Education Week’ celebrations in Hyderabad; also unveils ₹1,700-crore infrastructure works for integrated school complexes, additional classrooms and pre-primary sections in government schools

title: Most drowning cases occur in summer: Fire dept.
content: ‘Around 90% of drowning cases occur during summer vacation, and 95% of victims are students venturing into dangerous waters to swim or learn swimming’

title: 35 injured as bus, lorry collide in Aluva
content: The bus was heading from Aluva to Pookkattupady and the lorry was travelling in the opposite direction when they reportedly collided on a narrow road

title: CSML tasked with DPR for model road project
content: The road project was among the 21 priority initiatives identified by the new civic administration for its first 50 days in office. It will be implemented in three phases

title: Kochi metro’s Kakkanad extension likely to miss Dec. deadline
content: Uncertainty continues over securing the long-overdue ₹1,200-crore loan for the extension from the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank, which has in turn affected the pace of project execution

title: New excise regime comes into effect
content: Clarity on IML/ Beer prices by Tuesday while prices of some labels have been notified. No distribution of IML/ Beer through KSBCL on Monday

title: ECI set to roll out phase III of SIR in ‘coming days’; to cover 22 States, UTs
content: The poll authority had held back the exercise due to Assembly elections in Kerala, Assam, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu and West Bengal held last month

title: Indian Ocean more contested, and important than ever before: Vice Admiral Swaminathan
content: He observed that the Indian Ocean has always been India’s arena, and highlighted the growing significance of the region

title: West Bengal Chief Electoral Officer appointed State Chief Secretary
content: The decision of the BJP government under Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari to elevate the CEO to the top post in the State raised questions in political circles

title: PM Modi to meet Iran FM, other BRICS Ministers before UAE visit
content: Engagements indicate New Delhi is stepping up its global outreach amidst the war in West Asia and talks of economic austerity measures at home triggered by an energy crisis

title: Kerala Government formation: Impasse over chief ministerial candidate in Congress
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge and Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul held consultations with three main contenders on May 9, 2026

title: Woman found dead after fiancé’s death in bike accident
content: Prafulla reportedly expressed her wish to be buried at the same place where Mani was cremated in her suicide note

title: PM repeats call for saving fuel, cutting gold buy; bats for work from home
content: “We must make every effort to reduce the use of imported products and avoid personal activities that involve spending foreign currency,” PM Modi said

title: Police intensify probe into sexual assault at Kattakada bus station in Kerala, suspect still at large
content: According to the police, the suspect, identified as ‘Bruce Lee’ Shibu, is suspected to have fled to Tamil Nadu

title: Will hear Trinamool pleas on victory margins being less than deleted votes in SIR, says Supreme Court
content: This is the first Supreme Court hearing in the West Bengal SIR case after the election; in as many as 31 seats, the victory margins of the BJP were less than the number of votes deleted during the SIR, Trinamool leaders say

title: How a bid to make Thirumavalavan Tamil Nadu’s Chief Minister failed
content: The idea of making Mr. Thirumavalavan the CM was mooted after a bid for an AIADMK Government with DMK support hit hurdles, a senior VCK leader told The Hindu

title: Modi’s austerity drive is an admission of failure on economic and foreign policy fronts, alleges Akhilesh
content: It appears the BJP government has completely lost control; the dollar is skyrocketing, and the Indian rupee has plummeted, Mr. Yadav said

title: Himanta to be Assam’s first non-Congress CM twice in a row
content: Apart from Prime Minister Narendra Modi and some Union Ministers, ambassadors of a few countries, industrialists, and religious leaders will attend Tuesday’s oath-taking ceremony

title: Canara Bank posts a 10% decline in Q4 net profit
content: Canara Bank’s savings account deposits in the quarter increased 8.73% year-on-year to ₹3,66,581 crore

title: Former Bengal Minister Sujit Bose arrested by ED
content: The investigation agency said the Trinamool Congress leader had illegally recommended 150 candidates for different posts under the South Dum Dum Municipality in lieu of pecuniary benefits

title: CPI(M) defends Vijayan, says leadership is collective after Kerala defeat
content: General secretary M.A. Baby rules out ‘moral responsibility’ argument; no decision yet on Leader of Opposition post, as Left begins internal review

title: News Analysis | Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Crisis over CM choice lays bare fault lines in Cong.
content: Several senior Congress leaders and ordinary workers feel let down by the party’s insouciance. IUML, which spearheaded the UDF poll campaign successfully in many constituencies, has reason to be annoyed.  Congress’ central leadership should have anticipated the present scenario, more so with the  AICC general secretary in charge of the organisation being in contention, and acted pre-emptively by laying out its criteria for the chief ministerial choice

title: NEET-UG 2026 under scanner as Rajasthan SOG probes ‘guess paper’ leak
content: According to police, of 410 questions circulated, 120 chemistry questions matched those that appeared in final exam paper; no arrests has been made so far

title: VB-G RAM G to come into force on July 1: govt.
content: MGNREGA, enacted under the Congress-led UPA as a nationwide rural employment scheme with statutory backing for 100 days of guaranteed work, will also stand repealed on July 1; Union Minister says most States will have six months to complete the necessary transition phase

title: Iltija Mufti’s X account withheld, says ‘attempt to silence her’
content: X withheld Mufti’s social media handle with the message: ‘In response to a legal demand’; PDP’s spokesperson says silencing voices will not silence the issues

title: Green energy key to India’s future growth: Naidu
content: A.P. Chief Minister says energy security, decentralised power systems and sustainable development are vital for India’s economic transformation; pitches rooftop solar, micro-grids and natural farming at CII summit

title: Congress holds strategy meet over GBA polls, DKS says ‘new guarantees for Bengaluru’ on May 13
content: This announcement comes a day after Mr. Shivakumar, who is also Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) President, held a meeting with Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and All India Congress Committee (AICC) general secretary Randeep Surjewala, in charge of party affairs in the State on Sunday. Mr. Shivakumar described it as a ‘political meeting’

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly: Churning in AIADMK becomes evident with split over Leader of Legislature Party post
content: In the morning, a letter, purportedly signed by a group of AIADMK legislators stating that Mr. Palaniswami had been elected the Leader of the Legislature Party, was submitted to the Pro-tem Speaker M.V. Karuppaiah. In the evening, Mr. Shanmugam presented a letter claiming support for the leadership of Mr. Velumani.

title: SC places Sringeri recount result in abeyance, restores Congress leader as MLA for now
content: A Bench headed by Justice Sanjay Kumar ordered status quo ante while issuing notice to the BJP’s Jeevaraj and other respondents

title: Thirumavalavan’s dilemma over Cabinet berth stirs debate in VCK
content: Party sources said while Mr. Thirumavalavan does not want to formally join the State Government, the party’s office bearers are urging him to rethink it

title: VB-GRAM G replaces MGNREGA from July 1 | Key details explained
content: The Central Government has announced VB-GRAM G, a new rural employment mission set to replace MGNREGA from July 1, 2026. The transition aims to be smooth, with job cards remaining valid and ongoing work continuing without disruption. Here’s everything you need to know about the new scheme and what it means for rural workers.

title: Himanta Biswa Sarma resigns as Assam Chief Minister; swearing-in after May 11
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma says the meeting to elect the BJP Legislature Party leader will be held in the presence of Union Minister J.P. Nadda, and Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini

title: Nishant Kumar likely to be elected to Bihar Legislative Council next month
content: Out of 10 seats, nine are going to be vacated on June 28 while, one seat held by JD(U) president Nitish Kumar was already vacated when he got elected to the Rajya Sabha

title: Watch: Avoid gold, foreign trips & adopt WFH: How will it affect the economy? | Above the Fold | 11.05.2026
content: In today’s episode: Modi’s appeal amid the West Asia crisis, the suspense over the Congress’s Kerala Chief Minister pick, and the political buzz around Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay in Tamil Nadu.

title: GBA polls: Karnataka SEC appeals to Supreme Court for extension of deadline  by 3 months
